methods.description: Protocol for separating proteins based on isoelectric point and molecular weight using two-dimensional gel electrophoresis, followed by visualization and image analysis to study protein expression profiles.

steps.description: An equal amount of protein (200 lg) sample was mixed with fresh rehydration buffer (9 M Urea, 4 % CHAPS, 1 % DTT, 1 % IPG buffer, and trace amounts of bromophenol blue) to a total volume of 450 lL.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare protein sample for 2D gel electrophoresis by mixing with rehydration buffer
inputs: Rehydration buffer (9 M Urea, 4 % CHAPS, 1 % DTT, 1 % IPG buffer, bromophenol blue)
outputs: Protein sample mixed with rehydration buffer (450 µL)

steps.description: The protein samples were then added to the strip holder.
steps.searchable_summary: Load protein sample onto IEF strip holder

steps.description: The isoelectric focusing protocol was carried out at 20°C, with 50 lA per strip.
The strips were laid across the top of the gel, making sure that the gels were lying flush with one another.
steps.searchable_summary: Place IEF strip onto SDS-PAGE gel
outputs: Gel with IEF strip placed on top

steps.description: After the sealing solution cooled and solidified, the strip was moved to the electrophoresis apparatus (Ettan-DALT-Six system; GE healthcare; USA).
steps.searchable_summary: Seal IEF strip onto SDS-PAGE gel and transfer to electrophoresis apparatus
outputs: Electrophoresis apparatus loaded with gel/strip assembly

steps.description: Electrophoresis was carried out at 15°C, and the gel was run at 100 V for 45 min, followed by 200 V for 6-8 h (until the bromophenol blue band reached the bottom of gel).
The gel was visualized by silver staining as described previously (Shevchenko et al. 1996).
outputs: Stained gel with visible protein spots

steps.description: All gel images were processed by a three step procedure: (1) protein spot detection; (2) quantify volume of the selected spot; and (3) match grayscale values using Bio-Rad PDQuest 8.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: Process 2D gel images using Bio-Rad PDQuest software including spot detection, quantification, and matching
outputs: Processed gel data (detected spots, quantitative data, matched spots)

methods.method_name: Mass Spectrometry (MS) Analysis for Protein Identification
methods.description: Procedure for identifying the protein composition of selected spots from a 2-DE gel using mass spectrometry and subsequent bioinformatics analysis.

steps.description: Two protein spots (p1007 and p8000) in the 2-DE gel were selected to identify their protein constitution using of mass spectrometry (Fig. 5).
steps.searchable_summary: Select specific protein spots from 2D gel for mass spectrometry analysis
inputs: Criteria for spot selection (e.g., spot numbers p1007, p8000)

steps.description: Identify their protein constitution using of mass spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform mass spectrometry on selected protein spots

steps.description: According to Mascot Score Histogram, four proteins were found in spot p1007 (Table 2)... Only one protein was found in protein spot p8000...
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze mass spectrometry data using Mascot software/database to identify proteins
outputs: Protein identification results (list of identified proteins per spot, Mascot scores)

steps.description: None of these proteins are a documented allergenic protein. Only one protein was found in protein spot p8000: gi|29839547 (RecName: Full=Putative invertase inhibitor; AltName: Full=Pollen allergen Pla a 1; AltName: Allergen=Pla a 1; Flags: Precursor).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare identified proteins against known allergen databases
outputs: Classification of identified proteins as allergenic or non-allergenic

steps.description: The amino acid sequence of the Pla a 1 is: MKLSFSLCIF FFNLLLLLQA VISADIVQGT CKKVAQRSPN VNYDFCVKSL GADPKSHTAD LQ GLGVISAN LAIQHGSKIQ TFIGRILKSK VDPALK-KYLN 101DCVGLYADAK SSVQEAIADF KSKDYA SANV KMSAALDDSV TCEDGFKEKK GIVSPVT-KEN KDYVQLTAIS LAITKLLGA. This amino acid sequence further demonstrated the identity of the allergenic protein expressed by the Platanus pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze amino acid sequence of identified protein for confirmation/characterization
inputs: Amino acid sequence of identified protein (Pla a 1 sequence provided)
outputs: Sequence analysis results (e.g., confirmation of protein identity)
